activities_projects.title: Theme Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the major themes of the story
steps: Identify themes such as kindness, sacrifice, and social inequality
Explain each theme with examples from the story

activities_projects.title: Symbolism of the Statue
steps: Analyze what the statue represents in society
Explain how it changes during the story

activities_projects.title: Contrast Between Rich and Poor
activities_projects.objective: Analyze social inequality in the story
steps: Identify examples of rich and poor characters
Explain the contrast between their lives

activities_projects.title: Role of Sacrifice
activities_projects.objective: Understand sacrifice as a central idea
steps: Identify sacrifices made by the prince and the swallow
Explain their importance in the story

activities_projects.title: Interpretation of the Ending
activities_projects.objective: Understand the deeper meaning of the ending
steps: Read the ending carefully
Explain why God calls the lead heart and the swallow the most precious things

activities_projects.title: Narrative Style Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the storytelling style of Oscar Wilde
steps: Identify elements like dialogue, description, and narration
Write how they contribute to the story

activities_projects.title: Critical Character Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Compare the prince and the swallow
steps: List similarities and differences between the two characters
Write a short comparison paragraph

activities_projects.title: Rewriting the Ending
steps: Imagine a different ending for the story
Write it in 6–8 sentences

activities_projects.title: Moral Interpretation
steps: Identify the moral message
Explain how the characters demonstrate this moral

activities_projects.title: Literary Appreciation
activities_projects.objective: Appreciate the literary beauty of the story
steps: Identify one descriptive passage from the story
Explain why it is effective and meaningful
